original: The Digest is published, codifies the works of classical jurists, commissioned by Eastern Roman Emperor Justinian
Eighth Council of Toledo begins at the praetorian church of Saints Peter and Paul in Toledo, will issue the Liber Iudiciorum, a twelve-book law-code under Visigoth King Recceswinth
An Lushan revolts against Chancellor Yang Guozhong at Fanyang, initiating the An Shi Rebellion during Chinese Tang Dynasty
Marinus I begins his reign as Catholic Pope succeeding John VIII
Nanboku-chō, Emperor Go-Kameyama of Japan, abdicates in favor of rival claimant Go-Komatsu
Valdivia earthquake with estimated 8.5 magnitude takes place, first recorded earthquake in Chile
Danzig rebellion ends in a compromise between the city's nobility and Polish King Stephen Báthory, after the King restored and recognized the city's economic and religious privileges
Battle of Noryang Point: In the final battle of the Seven Year War, the Korean navy decisively defeats the Japanese
Spanish viceroy Hernando Arias de Saavedra founds provinces Rio de la Plata (Argentina)/Guaira (Paraguay)
Mount Vesuvius in Italy erupts, burying many villages under lava flows and killing around 3,000 people
General George Monck demands free parliamentary election in Scotland
English Parliament passes Bill of Rights establishing limits on crown powers and requirement for regular elections
Last recorded eruption of Mount Fuji in Japan.
Prussian Libya falls to Silezie
Seven Years' War: After four-month siege, Russian army under Pyotr Rumyantsev takes Prussian fortress of Kolobrzeg (German: Kolberg).
Van Ritter von Glucks opera "Alceste" premieres
Virginia becomes 1st state to ratify Articles of Confederation (US 1st constitution)
Earthquake hits New Madrid, Missouri, causing widespread damage
Leaders of Molukkas uprising hanged in Ambon
Great North Holland Canal opens
Benjamin W. Edwards rides into Mexican controlled Nacogdoches, Texas and declares himself ruler of the Republic of Fredonia.
Fire consumes over 600 buildings in NYC
Battle of Blood River: Zulu impis defeated by Voortrekkers in South Africa (Great Trek)
Ships the Charlotte-Jane and the Randolph bring the first of the Canterbury Pilgrims to Lyttelton, New Zealand
Earthquake in Naples, Italy
Dutch government decides to vacate Schokland Island
Frederiksborg Castle, largest Renaissance complex in Scandinavia, is badly damaged in a fire - later restored and turned into a museum
Kingdom of Nepal accepts its constitution
Battle of Nashville ends after 4400 causalities
Beginning of the First Boer War between British Empire and Boer South African Republic
Transvaal region declares itself as the Republic of South Africa
Tonkin Campaign: The French capture the citadel in Hanoi
Rift at Dutch Reformed Church over "Doleantie"
Negro Methodist Episcopal Church founded in Jackson, TN
1st submarine with an internal combustion engine demonstrated
Italian football club A.C. Milan founded as Milan Foot-Ball and Cricket Club by Englishmen Alfred Edwards and Herbert Kilpin (disputed possibly 13 Dec)
A National Civic Federation is formed in the US to arbitrate labor disputes
Boer army under gen Kritzinger take Cape colony
Boer General Kritzinger captured
Majestic Theater, NYC, becomes 1st in US to employ women ushers
The Taj Mahal Palace & Tower hotel opens its doors to guests for the first time, in Bombay, British India
"Variety" covering all phases of show business, 1st published
Eugenia Farrar is the 1st to sing over a wireless radio broadcast, at the Brooklyn Navy Yard, New York
1st credit union in US forms (Manchester NH)
A conservative revolution and US pressure forces Nicaraguan President Jose Santos from office
UK National Insurance Act becomes law, providing health insurance for industrial workers (about a third of the workforce)
1st US postage stamp picturing an airplane, 20 cent parcel post, issued
Austria-Hungary engage in conflict with Serbia
First Balkan War: The Ottoman Navy is defeated by the Royal Hellenic Navy at the Battle of Eli
German warships fire more than 500 shells on northern English towns of Scarborough, Whitby and Hartlepool - 1st time English civilians targeted during the war - kill 137 people
World War I: German battleships under Franz Von Hipper bombard the English ports of Hartlepool and Scarborough.
German troops evacuate Finland, give over Kiev in the Ukraine to revolutionary forces and pull back from Estonia as Bolshevik forces move in
8.5 earthquake rocks the Gansu province in China, killing an estimated 200,000
The Communist Party of South Africa (CPSA) calls for a united front in a pass burning campaign on Dingaan's Day
Mutual Association of Eastern Colored Baseball Clubs formally organizes
NSW all out for 786 against South Australia Cricket
Alpha Phi Omega national service fraternity is founded at Lafayette College.
WOW-AM in Omaha NE begins radio transmissions
Chic Blackhawks 1st game at Chicago Stadium, beat Pittsburgh Pirates, 3-1
German SPD begins Iron Front against fascism
Abe de Vries & Sipke Castelein win Elfstedentocht
John Monks and Fred Finklehoff's play "Brother Rat" premieres in New York City
Theodore Cole and Ralph Roe attempt to escape from American federal prison Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay; neither is ever seen again.
State of California executes Ed Davis (38), leader of Folsom Prison escape attempt resulting in murder of warden and guard (San Quentin State Prison, Marin County)
WWII: British air raid on Mannheim, Germany
Sarawak occupied by Japanese forces
Hitlerorders combat against partisans in Russia and the Balkans
"Tamiami Champion" (New York to Florida) trains collide, kills 73 & injures 200
German V-2 strikes Antwerp bioscope (638 kill)
Nazi Germany launches a counteroffensive against the Allies in the Ardennes region of Belgium, beginning the 'Battle of the Bulge'
US 2nd Infantry division occupies "Heartbreak Crossroads" Wahlerscheid
1947 NFL Draft: Bob Fenimore from University of Oklahoma A&M first pick by Chicago Bears
Thailand joins the United Nations
NBC premiere of "Dragnet" in black and white
Charles E. Yeager flies > 2,575 kph in Bell X-1A
Sir Feroz Khan Noon replaces Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigar as Prime Minister of Pakistan
Bogota warehouse fire kills 82
Snow falling in Lowarai Pass West Pakistan kills 48
TWA 266 & United 826 collide over Staten Island, kills 134
Nepal establishes its constitution and becomes a Constitutional Hindu Monarchy
NY Giant Y. A. Tittle sets NFL season touchdown pass record at 33 with 6 touchdowns vs Dallas (41-31)
US performs nuclear test at Pacific Ocean
Gemini 6 returns to Earth
Pioneer 6 launched into solar orbit
Taufa'ahau Tupou IV becomes King of Tonga
The Beatles release "Everywhere it's Christmas" in UK
"Playtime", French film directed by Jacques Tati, starring himself, is released
KFIZ TV channel 34 in Fond du Lac, Wisconsin begins broadcasting
Official revocation of the Edict of Expulsion of Jews from Spain at Second Vatican Council
British House of Commons votes 343-185 to abolish the death penalty
"Love Story" film based on the novel by Erich Segal, directed by Arthur Hiller and starring Ryan O'Neal and Ali McGraw is released
Polish Vice-Prime Minister Stanisław Kociołek, condemns labor protesters and calls for the workers to get back to work, in a televised speech
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Don McLean's 8+ minute version of "American Pie" released
Vijay Diwas (Victory Day): East Pakistan's independence (as Bangladesh) from Pakistan is recognized internationally after India's army occupies Dacca, and 93,000 West Pakistani troops surrender
Bangladesh Constitution goes into effect
Miami Dolphins become 1st undefeated NFL team (14-0-0)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USSR performs nuclear test at Eastern Kazakh, Semipalitinsk USSR
1st broadcast of "One Day at a Time" on CBS TV
Andrew Young named Ambassador & Chief US Delegate to UN
Government halts swine flu vaccination program following reports of paralysis
Liberian tanker stranded at Nantucket, 180,000 barrels oil in sea
Cleveland, Ohio becomes the first post-Depression era city to default on its loans, owing $14,000,000 to local banks.
4 British Army soldiers are killed by a PIRA landmine near Dungannon, County Tyrone. Another British Army soldier was killed by a PIRA landmine near Forkill, County Armagh
Dallas QB Roger Staubach throws 3 TDs and 336 yards in his last NFL regular season game with the Cowboys, a 35-34 win over Washington Redskins at Texas Stadium
Libya raises crude oil prices, joining four other OPEC nations, which has a dramatic effect on the United States
President-electRonald Reaganannounces Alexander Haig Jr. as 59th Secretary of State
Dutch Van Agt's 2nd government falls
The Federal Reserve announces that the operating capacity of factories has gone down to 67.8%.
Riverside, California judge denies cerebral palsy victim Elizabeth Bouviato request to starve herself to death in a county hospital
Spokesperson for rock band "The Who" announces the group is disbanding
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51-L mission
Roh Tae Woo elected president of South Korea
American political cult leader Lyndon LaRouche convicted of tax and mail fraud
Commencement of 1st Test Cricket play at Bellerive Oval, Hobart (v SL)
Geoff Marsh completes 355* for WA against South Australia
Protest breaks out in Timişoara in response to an attempt by the government to evict dissident Hungarian pastor, László Tőkés.
KUSW, Salt Lake City Utah, final shortwave radio transmissions
Rev. Jean-Bertrand Aristide elected President of Haiti
Independence of the Republic of Kazakhstan.
MLB expansion club Florida Marlins sign their 1st player, 16-year-old pitcher Clemente Nunez
UN reverses ruling that Zionism is racism by 111-25 (13 abstain) vote
The official adoption of the name "Euro"
Iraq disarmament crisis: Operation Desert Fox - the United States and United Kingdom bomb targets in Iraq to degrade its ability to produce, store, maintain, and deliver weapons of mass destruction
NASA's Voyager 1 spacecraft is the 1st to cross the termination shock, where solar and interstellar winds merge
4 people are killed after Cyclone Evan strikes Fiji
A gang rape of a woman on a bus in India that resulted in her death leads to national and international outrage

21 people are killed and 20 are injured after a bus falls from an elevated highway in Manila, Philippines
79th Heisman Trophy Award: Jameis Winston, Florida State (QB)

5 day pollution red alert declared in Beijing and 21 other Chinese cities, classified by officials as a “meteorological disaster”
Huge explosion and fire in a restaurant in Sapporo, Japan injures 42
Sri Lanka's Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe is sworn back into office two months after being removed by President Sirisena
14 people found guilty of 2015 terror attacks on Charlie Hebdo office and supermarket in Paris
China blocks imports of coal, from Australia, the world's second biggest market for the world's biggest coal exporter
Major winter storm hits US Northeast resulting in at least seven deaths and Binghamton, New York, receiving record 41 inches of snow
Nine-year-old girl who died of an asthma attack in 2013 becomes first person in the world to officially have air pollution listed as cause of death
US Major League Baseball announces it is elevating the Negro Leagues to Major League status
Six children die in a bouncy castle accident at a school in Devonport, Tasmasia, Australia

Japan announces its biggest military build-up since WWII, amid concerns over China and North Korea
Landslide sweeps through a campsite in Batang Kali township, Selangor state, Malaysia, killing at least 21 people during the night
Pokemon producers announce main man Ash Ketchum and his Pikachu will leave the anime series after 25 years
61 people drown after a boat carrying migrants capsizes off the coast Zuwara, Libya
New research indicates 37 Bronze-Age Britons were butchered and cannibalized and their remains thrown down a shaft in  Charterhouse Warren in England
Rare school shooting in Wisconsin leaves 1 teacher and one student dead with others injured - rare case of a female carrying out a mass shooting
KingHenry VI of Englandaged seven is crowned King of France at Notre Dame, Paris (only English monarch to wear both crowns)

Revolt in Kazakhstan against Communist party, known as Zheltoksan, which becomes the first sign of ethnic strife duringGorbachev's tenure

Mariah Carey's single "All I Want For Christmas Is You" reaches No. 1, 25 years after release
German ChancellorOlaf Scholz, at the head of a minority government, loses a no-confidence vote in parliament, making way for early elections
Boston tea party incident - Sons of Liberty protesters throw tea shipments into Boston harbor in protest against British imposed Tea Act
